statement: MCP tool schema descriptions are the primary signal an LLM uses to decide when and how to invoke a tool. Invest in clear, specific description strings — vague descriptions cause the model to skip the tool or pass wrong arguments. Include: what the tool does, when to use it vs alternatives, required vs optional params, and one concrete example in the description.

evidenceRefs.title: Model Context Protocol specification — Tool definition
Anthropic — Building effective agents (tool design guidance)

publicSummary: MCP tool descriptions are the LLM's primary invocation signal. Make them specific: what it does, when to use, params, and an example.
cautionNotes: Tool descriptions consume context tokens — keep them dense but not verbose.
